_description: Geographic hierarchy (countries, regions), operational facilities (sites), and structured address infrastructure. Layer 0 block that is independently deployable.
_organizations_note: Organizations defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.
_tenants_note: Tenants defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.

description.en: Classifies sites by operational function: Office, Warehouse, Plant, Distribution Center, Data Center, Retail, Virtual
Purpose classification for addresses: Home, Work, Billing, Shipping, Legal, Mailing, Registered, Delivery
A sovereign country or territory identified by ISO 3166 code. Defines geographic boundary for regulatory compliance, currency, and tax jurisdiction.
A state, province, or administrative region within a country. Used for tax calculations, shipping zones, and regional compliance.
A physical facility -- office, warehouse, plant, distribution center, or virtual location where work happens, goods are stored, or services are delivered.
A structured postal/physical address. Reusable by any entity via aggregation or association. Carries street, city, postal code, coordinates, and links to geographic hierarchy.
Aggregated site data across dimensions like country and site type
Aggregated site data by active/inactive status and site type
Aggregated address data across dimensions like country and address type
Country contains regions. Aggregation: regions are reference data that varies by deployment.
Site contains its address(es). Aggregation: addresses require authored content, not auto-instantiated.
Site is located in a region. Attribution: site has independent identity.
Classifies a site by its operational function. Every site has exactly one type.
Classifies an address by purpose (Home, Work, Billing, Shipping, etc.). An address has exactly one type.
Links an address to the geographic hierarchy. Optional: not all addresses map to the enterprise region hierarchy.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., US, GB, DE, JP, BR)
Official name of the country
ISO 4217 currency code for the country's primary legal tender (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP)
ISO 3166-2 subdivision code or locally defined code (e.g., US-CA, GB-ENG)
Name of the state, province, or administrative region
Whether this region is currently active for operations
Unique identifier code for the site
Human-readable name for the site
Whether this site is currently operational
Primary capacity metric. Unit depends on site type: headcount for offices, pallet positions for warehouses, production units/day for plants.
Primary street address line
Secondary address line (suite, apartment, floor, building)
City or municipality name
State, province, or region code. Should align with attributed erp-region.
Postal / ZIP code. Format varies by country.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Denormalized from geographic hierarchy for convenience.
Geographic latitude for mapping, distance calculations, and logistics
Geographic longitude
Whether this is the primary address for the owning entity
Whether this address is currently valid. Allows historical addresses to be kept without deletion.
When this address became effective. Enables temporal address tracking.
Country dimension for site distribution aggregation
Site type dimension for site distribution aggregation
Count of sites per country per site type
Sum of site capacity per country per site type
Active/inactive status dimension
Site type dimension for status aggregation
Count of sites per status per type
Country code dimension for address distribution
Address type dimension for address distribution
Count of addresses per country per address type
Event emitted when a new site is created
Event emitted when a site is deactivated
Event emitted when a new address is created
Geographic hierarchy, operational facilities, and structured address infrastructure for the ERP ontology. Layer 0 block.
Manages countries, regions, and geographic hierarchy.
Manages operational facilities with type classification and region attribution.
Manages structured postal addresses with type classification and geographic linking.
Geographic footprint: site count and total capacity by country and site type. Identifies concentration risk and coverage gaps.
Facility lifecycle: count of sites by active/inactive status and type. Useful for facilities planning and real estate portfolio management.
Geographic spread of all addressable entities by country and address type. Identifies operational reach and market coverage.
